sections.Description:
The Psion Tyrant does not buff Vek and instead damages all player-controlled units every turn, including theRenfield Bomband deployable tanks. It has a primarily orange body with dark purple bumps adorning its head. Psion Tyrants only spawn on theVolcanic Hiveand are capped at one spawn per "phase" of the final mission.
Under thePsionic Receiver, the Psion Tyrant also inflicts 1 damage to all Vek, the Tyrant itself included.
sections.Strategy:
While the Tyrant Psion can be very dangerous to mechs with low health (usually artillery or science mechs), or mechs with self-damaging weapons, it's of no direct threat to the grid, or to mechs that have armor or a healthy amount of spare healthbar. If there are any spare cores leftover at the end of the game, placing them in +2 HP upgrades may help in order to assist with ignoring the Tyrant until a more convenient turn, so as to take care of more direct threats.
The Tyrant Psion itself is relatively weak and should be killable via collateral damage or fire. Overall it's likely better to see this Psion emerge from a spawnpoint than any other normal enemy.
Remember to take into account that a live Tyrant Psion, on phase 2 of the final battle, will attack the Renfield bomb along with your units, so factor that into the amount of damage the bomb is "allowed to take". If you have any deployable tanks, it will attack those too, so upgrading them to 3HP may also help.

Description
[
]
The Psion Tyrant does not buff Vek and instead damages all player-controlled units every turn, including the
Renfield Bomb
and deployable tanks. It has a primarily orange body with dark purple bumps adorning its head. Psion Tyrants only spawn on the
Volcanic Hive
and are capped at one spawn per "phase" of the final mission.
Under the
Psionic Receiver
, the Psion Tyrant also inflicts 1 damage to all Vek, the Tyrant itself included.
Strategy
[
]
While the Tyrant Psion can be very dangerous to mechs with low health (usually artillery or science mechs), or mechs with self-damaging weapons, it's of no direct threat to the grid, or to mechs that have armor or a healthy amount of spare healthbar. If there are any spare cores leftover at the end of the game, placing them in +2 HP upgrades may help in order to assist with ignoring the Tyrant until a more convenient turn, so as to take care of more direct threats.
The Tyrant Psion itself is relatively weak and should be killable via collateral damage or fire. Overall it's likely better to see this Psion emerge from a spawnpoint than any other normal enemy.
Remember to take into account that a live Tyrant Psion, on phase 2 of the final battle, will attack the Renfield bomb along with your units, so factor that into the amount of damage the bomb is "allowed to take". If you have any deployable tanks, it will attack those too, so upgrading them to 3HP may also help.
